title: Hope PSBank partners with JAMB to provide e-pin purchase to candidates
description: Hope PSBank has announced that prospective candidates for the upcoming 2021 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination can now purchase the e-pin on the Bank’s platform, by simply visiting www.hopepsb
html:
Hope PSBank has announced that prospective candidates for the upcoming 2021 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination can now purchase the e-pin on the Bank’s platform, by simply visiting www.hopepsbank.com or any Hope PSBank Agent Outlet.

To purchase the e-pin, candidates/parents should visit Hope PSBank’s website or Mobile App:  SELECT PAYMENTS, SELECT ACCOUNT TO DEBIT, SELECT EDUCATION, SELECT JAMB, SELECT E-PIN DE or UTME, ENTER PROFILE CODE and authorize with your transaction PIN. The transaction will be approved and an e-pin will be generated. The e-pin generated can be used on the JAMB website.

The profile code can be retrieved via SMS by sending candidates NIN details to 55019 in the following format; NIN space NIN number.

Prospective candidates who have not registered for the National Identification Number (NIN) which is mandatory for the UTME  can have this done by visiting the Hope PSBank website (www.hopepsbank.com): select NIN Registration and fill the registration form then proceed to any Hope PSBankNIN Enrolment Center spread across the country for data capturing. This eliminates the need to visit multiple centres to register for NIN and purchase JAMB e-pins.

Candidates who have their NIN can visit any Hope PSBank agent and make payment for the JAMB e-pin which would cost N4000 or pay directly via Hope PSBank Digital App or Internet Banking. The e-Pin purchase portal is expected to close on Friday 10th of May.

According to Cletus Igah, Group Head, Corporate Services: “Hope PSBank, Nigeria’s Premier Digital Bank is dedicated to providing innovative solutions that cut across the financial market and also provide access to financial services via digital platforms to all cadres of Nigerians”

Since its inception in 2019, Hope Payment Service Bank, Nigeria’s premier digital bank has continually set the pace for driving financial inclusion, digital economy, and the cashless policy by providing innovative digital banking services.
